forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,31 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KTBNM0YGVRJQJSCTQBDHR68H
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: 8b80
+project: local-review
+title: 'Engine stage 3 — verify: adversarial refute pass over candidate findings'
+---
+## What
+The differentiator from ultra-review: a candidate finding is reported only after an independent agent tries to REFUTE it against the real code and fails. This converts "possible issues" into "confirmed issues" and kills the noise that makes the current review untrustworthy.
+
+- Input: `Vec<Finding>` from the fleet. For each finding, spawn a verifier agent (via `execute_agents`, parallel) whose mandate is to disprove the claim using the tools: re-run `find duplicates`/`search symbol` to confirm a duplicate really exists; re-run inbound `get callgraph`/`get blastradius` to confirm "dead code" truly has no callers and isn't an entry point/export/test; read surrounding code to confirm a correctness/security claim actually holds.
+- The verifier returns `VerifiedFinding { finding, confirmed, reason }` and DEFAULTS TO `confirmed = false` (refuted) on uncertainty or tool failure — only positively-substantiated findings survive.
+- Run verification concurrently across findings with the same adaptive-concurrency primitive. Cap fan-out (a huge candidate list shouldn't spawn unbounded agents) and `log()` any cap applied so silent truncation never reads as "all verified."
+- Output: `Vec<VerifiedFinding>` (all findings, with verdicts) so synthesis can drop refuted ones and the summary can report how many were filtered.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `verify_findings(findings, connection, opts) -> Vec<VerifiedFinding>` exists; one verifier per finding, run concurrently, capped with a logged cap.
+- [ ] The verifier prompt is adversarial (refute-by-default) and instructs re-probing with the dimension-appropriate code_context tools.
+- [ ] Uncertain / tool-failure verdicts resolve to `confirmed = false`.
+- [ ] Confirmed and refuted counts are available to the caller.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Mock-agent test: 3 candidate findings, scripted verifier responses (2 confirm, 1 refute) → result carries the correct verdicts; a 4th finding whose verifier errors resolves to refuted.
+- [ ] Cap test: more findings than the concurrency cap still all get verified (queued), and the applied cap is logged.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators review::verify` green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — script confirm/refute/error verifier responses and assert verdicts first. Reuse `execute_agents`; the only new content is the adversarial verifier prompt template and the refute-by-default parsing.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -9,23 +9,25 @@
 title: 'Engine stage 3 — verify: adversarial refute pass over candidate findings'
 ---
 ## What
-The differentiator from ultra-review: a candidate finding is reported only after an independent agent tries to REFUTE it against the real code and fails. This converts "possible issues" into "confirmed issues" and kills the noise that makes the current review untrustworthy.
+The differentiator from ultra-review: a candidate finding is reported only after it survives an independent refutation against ground-truth probe evidence. This converts "possible issues" into "confirmed issues" and kills the noise that makes the current review untrustworthy. Verification has two layers — a cheap deterministic guard, then an adversarial agent.

-- Input: `Vec<Finding>` from the fleet. For each finding, spawn a verifier agent (via `execute_agents`, parallel) whose mandate is to disprove the claim using the tools — re-running the same probes the source validator declared: re-run `find_duplicates`/`search_symbol` to confirm a duplicate really exists; re-run inbound `get_callgraph`/`get_blastradius` to confirm "dead code" truly has no callers and isn't an entry point/export/test; read surrounding code to confirm a correctness/security claim holds.
-- The verifier returns `VerifiedFinding { finding, confirmed, reason }` and DEFAULTS TO `confirmed = false` (refuted) on uncertainty or tool failure — only positively-substantiated findings survive.
-- Run verification concurrently with the same adaptive-concurrency primitive. Cap fan-out (a huge candidate list shouldn't spawn unbounded agents) and `log()` any cap applied so silent truncation never reads as "all verified."
-- Output: `Vec<VerifiedFinding>` (all findings, with verdicts) so synthesis can drop refuted ones and the summary can report how many were filtered.
+1. **Probe guard (deterministic, no LLM).** Re-use the structured `probe_results` (and re-run the one relevant probe for the exact finding via the probe registry) to auto-refute findings that contradict ground truth: a `dead-code` claim is dropped if the inbound callgraph shows callers; a "duplicate" claim is dropped if `find_duplicates` returns no match for that block. This catches agent hallucinations for free, before spending an agent.
+2. **Adversarial verifier (agent).** For each finding that survives the guard, spawn a verifier agent (via `execute_agents`, parallel) whose mandate is to DISPROVE the claim using the injected probe evidence + reading surrounding code. It returns `VerifiedFinding { finding, confirmed, reason }` and DEFAULTS TO `confirmed = false` on uncertainty or tool failure — only positively-substantiated findings survive.
+
+- Run verification concurrently with the adaptive-concurrency primitive. Cap fan-out and `log()` any cap applied so silent truncation never reads as "all verified."
+- Output: `Vec<VerifiedFinding>` (all candidates, with verdicts + which layer refuted) so synthesis can drop refuted ones and the summary reports how many were filtered.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `verify_findings(findings, connection, opts) -> Vec<VerifiedFinding>` exists; one verifier per finding, run concurrently, capped with a logged cap.
-- [ ] The verifier prompt is adversarial (refute-by-default) and re-runs the source validator's probes.
-- [ ] Uncertain / tool-failure verdicts resolve to `confirmed = false`.
-- [ ] Confirmed and refuted counts are available to the caller.
+- [ ] `verify_findings(findings, work_list, connection, opts) -> Vec<VerifiedFinding>` exists; applies the deterministic probe guard first, then the adversarial agent on survivors.
+- [ ] The probe guard auto-refutes findings that contradict the structured probe evidence (e.g. dead-code with callers).
+- [ ] The verifier prompt is adversarial (refute-by-default); uncertain/tool-failure verdicts resolve to `confirmed = false`.
+- [ ] Confirmed/refuted counts and the refuting layer are available to the caller.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Mock-agent test: 3 candidate findings, scripted verifier responses (2 confirm, 1 refute) → correct verdicts; a 4th finding whose verifier errors resolves to refuted.
-- [ ] Cap test: more findings than the concurrency cap still all get verified (queued), and the applied cap is logged.
+- [ ] Guard test (no agent): a planted dead-code finding whose callgraph probe shows callers is auto-refuted; a real one passes the guard.
+- [ ] Mock-agent test: 3 guard-surviving findings, scripted verifier responses (2 confirm, 1 refute) → correct verdicts; a 4th whose verifier errors resolves to refuted.
+- [ ] Cap test: more findings than the concurrency cap still all get verified (queued), cap logged.
 - [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators review::verify` green.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — script confirm/refute/error verifier responses and assert verdicts first. Reuse `execute_agents`; the only new content is the adversarial verifier prompt template and the refute-by-default parsing.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — write the deterministic guard test first (it needs no agent), then the scripted confirm/refute/error verifier test. Reuse the probe registry and `execute_agents`; the only new content is the guard logic and the adversarial verifier prompt.
\ No newline at end of file

-- [ ] The probe guard auto-refutes findings that contradict the structured probe evidence (e.g. dead-code with callers).
-- [ ] The verifier prompt is adversarial (refute-by-default); uncertain/tool-failure verdicts resolve to `confirmed = false`.

-- Use `/tdd` — write the deterministic guard test first (it needs no agent), then the scripted confirm/refute/error verifier test. Reuse the probe registry and `execute_agents`; the only new content is the guard logic and the adversarial verifier prompt.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — script confirm/refute/error verifier responses and assert verdicts first. Reuse `execute_agents`; the only new content is the adversarial verifier prompt template and the refute-by-default parsing.

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -9,25 +9,27 @@
 title: 'Engine stage 3 — verify: adversarial refute pass over candidate findings'
 ---
 ## What
-The differentiator from ultra-review: a candidate finding is reported only after it survives an independent refutation against ground-truth probe evidence. This converts "possible issues" into "confirmed issues" and kills the noise that makes the current review untrustworthy. Verification has two layers — a cheap deterministic guard, then an adversarial agent.
+The differentiator from ultra-review: a candidate finding is reported only after it survives refutation against ground-truth evidence. Two layers — a cheap deterministic guard, then an adversarial agent — and both are **pipelined on the shared agent pool**, not a separate batch after fan-out.

-1. **Probe guard (deterministic, no LLM).** Re-use the structured `probe_results` (and re-run the one relevant probe for the exact finding via the probe registry) to auto-refute findings that contradict ground truth: a `dead-code` claim is dropped if the inbound callgraph shows callers; a "duplicate" claim is dropped if `find_duplicates` returns no match for that block. This catches agent hallucinations for free, before spending an agent.
-2. **Adversarial verifier (agent).** For each finding that survives the guard, spawn a verifier agent (via `execute_agents`, parallel) whose mandate is to DISPROVE the claim using the injected probe evidence + reading surrounding code. It returns `VerifiedFinding { finding, confirmed, reason }` and DEFAULTS TO `confirmed = false` on uncertainty or tool failure — only positively-substantiated findings survive.
+1. **Probe guard (deterministic, no worker).** As each fan-out finding comes back, run the guard inline. It **reuses** the `probe_results` already attached to the work item (no re-run) and acts only on **`fact` probes**:
+   - `callers` (fact) → auto-refute a `dead-code` finding whose symbol has non-empty inbound callers.
+   - `duplicates` (fact) → auto-refute a `duplication` finding with no matching duplicate block.
+   - `similar` is a **candidate** probe, not a fact → findings backed by `similar` (reuse-miss) get NO deterministic guard; they go straight to the agent. (Anything the guard can't decide passes through.)
+2. **Adversarial verifier (agent, pipelined).** For each finding that survives the guard, submit a verify task to the **same `AgentPool`** (so verification of early findings runs while later validators are still fanning out). The verifier prompt is the inverse of fan-out: the specific finding + that file's `source_slice` + the relevant `probe_results` + "try to DISPROVE this claim." It returns `VerifiedFinding { finding, confirmed, reason }` and DEFAULTS TO `confirmed = false` on uncertainty or tool failure — only positively-substantiated findings survive.

-- Run verification concurrently with the adaptive-concurrency primitive. Cap fan-out and `log()` any cap applied so silent truncation never reads as "all verified."
-- Output: `Vec<VerifiedFinding>` (all candidates, with verdicts + which layer refuted) so synthesis can drop refuted ones and the summary reports how many were filtered.
+- No separate concurrency control here — submission goes to the shared pool (task 5), worker count is the single knob.
+- Record which layer refuted (guard vs agent) on the `VerifiedFinding` so synthesis/summary can report confirmed/refuted counts and reasons.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `verify_findings(findings, work_list, connection, opts) -> Vec<VerifiedFinding>` exists; applies the deterministic probe guard first, then the adversarial agent on survivors.
-- [ ] The probe guard auto-refutes findings that contradict the structured probe evidence (e.g. dead-code with callers).
-- [ ] The verifier prompt is adversarial (refute-by-default); uncertain/tool-failure verdicts resolve to `confirmed = false`.
-- [ ] Confirmed/refuted counts and the refuting layer are available to the caller.
+- [ ] The deterministic guard reuses work-item `probe_results` (no re-run), auto-refutes only via `fact` probes (`callers`/`duplicates`), and passes `similar`-backed and undecidable findings through to the agent.
+- [ ] Surviving findings are verified by an adversarial agent submitted to the shared `AgentPool` (pipelined with fan-out), refute-by-default; uncertain/tool-failure → `confirmed = false`.
+- [ ] `VerifiedFinding` carries the verdict + reason + refuting layer; confirmed/refuted counts available to the caller.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Guard test (no agent): a planted dead-code finding whose callgraph probe shows callers is auto-refuted; a real one passes the guard.
+- [ ] Guard test (no agent): a planted `dead-code` finding whose `callers` result shows callers is auto-refuted; a real one passes; a `similar`-backed reuse-miss finding always passes the guard to the agent.
 - [ ] Mock-agent test: 3 guard-surviving findings, scripted verifier responses (2 confirm, 1 refute) → correct verdicts; a 4th whose verifier errors resolves to refuted.
-- [ ] Cap test: more findings than the concurrency cap still all get verified (queued), cap logged.
+- [ ] Pipelining test: verify tasks submitted while fan-out tasks are still in flight are processed by the same pool (no separate stage barrier before synthesis).
 - [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators review::verify` green.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write the deterministic guard test first (it needs no agent), then the scripted confirm/refute/error verifier test. Reuse the probe registry and `execute_agents`; the only new content is the guard logic and the adversarial verifier prompt.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — write the deterministic guard test first (needs no agent), then the scripted confirm/refute/error verifier test, then the pipelining test. Reuse the `AgentPool` and the probe `fact`/`candidate` kinds; the only new content is the guard logic and the adversarial verifier prompt.
\ No newline at end of file

-- No separate concurrency control here — submission goes to the shared pool (task 5), worker count is the single knob.
-- Record which layer refuted (guard vs agent) on the `VerifiedFinding` so synthesis/summary can report confirmed/refuted counts and reasons.
+- Run verification concurrently with the adaptive-concurrency primitive. Cap fan-out and `log()` any cap applied so silent truncation never reads as "all verified."
+- Output: `Vec<VerifiedFinding>` (all candidates, with verdicts + which layer refuted) so synthesis can drop refuted ones and the summary reports how many were filtered.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] The deterministic guard reuses work-item `probe_results` (no re-run), auto-refutes only via `fact` probes (`callers`/`duplicates`), and passes `similar`-backed and undecidable findings through to the agent.
-- [ ] Surviving findings are verified by an adversarial agent submitted to the shared `AgentPool` (pipelined with fan-out), refute-by-default; uncertain/tool-failure → `confirmed = false`.
-- [ ] `VerifiedFinding` carries the verdict + reason + refuting layer; confirmed/refuted counts available to the caller.
+- [ ] `verify_findings(findings, work_list, connection, opts) -> Vec<VerifiedFinding>` exists; applies the deterministic probe guard first, then the adversarial agent on survivors.
+- [ ] The probe guard auto-refutes findings that contradict the structured probe evidence (e.g. dead-code with callers).
+- [ ] The verifier prompt is adversarial (refute-by-default); uncertain/tool-failure verdicts resolve to `confirmed = false`.
+- [ ] Confirmed/refuted counts and the refuting layer are available to the caller.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Guard test (no agent): a planted `dead-code` finding whose `callers` result shows callers is auto-refuted; a real one passes; a `similar`-backed reuse-miss finding always passes the guard to the agent.
+- [ ] Guard test (no agent): a planted dead-code finding whose callgraph probe shows callers is auto-refuted; a real one passes the guard.
 - [ ] Mock-agent test: 3 guard-surviving findings, scripted verifier responses (2 confirm, 1 refute) → correct verdicts; a 4th whose verifier errors resolves to refuted.
-- [ ] Pipelining test: verify tasks submitted while fan-out tasks are still in flight are processed by the same pool (no separate stage barrier before synthesis).
+- [ ] Cap test: more findings than the concurrency cap still all get verified (queued), cap logged.
 - [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators review::verify` green.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write the deterministic guard test first (needs no agent), then the scripted confirm/refute/error verifier test, then the pipelining test. Reuse the `AgentPool` and the probe `fact`/`candidate` kinds; the only new content is the guard logic and the adversarial verifier prompt.
